data.title: Bitcoin ETF ARK 21Shares sees largest outflows since August due to tariffs and $19B liquidation, says analysts.
data.description: Experts point to the impact of tariffs and a recent $19 billion liquidation event as the main reasons for institutional investors shifting towards defensive strategies.

data.title: SLB's Q3 2025 revenue hit $8.9B, up 4% QoQ but down 3% YoY. Pre-tax income slumped by 22% to $1B. Net income dropped 27% to $739M.
data.description: Houston-based SLB (NYSE: SLB) unveiled its third-quarter 2025 financial results. The revenue for the quarter was $8.928 billion, marking a 4% increase from the previous quarter but a 3% decrease compared to the same period last year. Income before taxes on a GAAP basis was reported at $1 billion, showing a 22% decrease from the previous quarter and a 34% decrease from the same period last year. The income before taxes margin on a GAAP basis was 11.2%, down by 383 basis points sequentially and 525 basis points year-on-year. Net income attributable to SLB on a GAAP basis was $739 million, down by 27% from the previous quarter.
SLB Q3 2025 Earnings - Rev. $8.93B (est $8.9B) - Adj EPS 69C (est 66C) - Adj. EBITDA $2.06B (est $2.07B) - Well Construction Rev. $2.91B (est $2.93B) - Capex $409M (est $503.5M)

data.title: Colossal's Toddler of the Year Campaign raises $12.7 million for Toys for Tots, 3-year-old winner receives $25,000.
data.description: In the 2025 Hollywood Christmas Parade, three-year-old Dominik "Domi" Gergely emerged victorious, bagging a $25,000 prize and garnering significant attention. The contest, organized by Colossal, the top professional fundraising entity in the country, has come to a close. This victory has marked Domi as the Toddler of the Year, propelling him into the limelight.

data.title: CrossAmerica Partners set to disclose Q3 2025 earnings on Nov 5.
data.description: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a concerning 10%, with approximately 15 million individuals currently out of work. This marks a significant increase from the previous year's rate of 6%. Additionally, the report highlighted that the economy shrank by 5% in the last quarter, resulting in a GDP of $20 trillion. The government has announced plans to allocate $50 billion towards creating jobs and stimulating economic growth in the coming months. The report also indicated that inflation has risen by 2.5%, impacting the cost of goods and services across the nation.Experts are warning that swift action is needed to address these economic challenges and prevent further damage to the country's financial stability.

data.title: Global Journey of Elegant Driving (China) by LEPAS ends in Wuhu, Anhui, by Chery Group's NEV brand, on Oct 16.
data.description: On October 16, 2025, in Wuhu, Anhui, the Global Journey of Elegant Driving (China Stop) by Chery Group's new NEV brand LEPAS was completed. The event saw participants traveling through busy cities and peaceful natural scenery, signaling the beginning of LEPAS's brand legacy.

data.title: Carter’s, Inc. launches OshKosh B’gosh Reissued Collection, bringing back iconic styles for families. #childhood #denim #overalls.
data.description: Carter’s, Inc. (NYSE: CRI) based in Atlanta, introduces The OshKosh Reissued Collection, commemorating the iconic World’s Best Overalls™ and childhood denim heritage. As North America's leading apparel company for babies and young children, Carter’s unveils this ongoing limited collection, reissuing select timeless OshKosh B’gosh styles for nostalgic families. This launch celebrates the brand's legacy with a modern twist, appealing to long-time fans and new customers alike.

data.title: Ferguson Enterprises Inc. reports Director shares vesting from Long Term Incentive Plan 2019, Ordinary Share Plan 2019, and Performance Ordinary Share Plan 2019.
data.description: In Newport News, Virginia, there has been a recent announcement regarding transactions by Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities (“PDMRs”) involving common stock of par value $0.0001 each in the company. This pertains to the vesting of the Ferguson Enterprises Inc. Long Term Incentive Plan 2019 (“LTIP”), Ferguson Enterprises Inc. Ordinary Share Plan 2019 (“OSP”), and Ferguson Enterprises Inc. Performance Ordinary Share Plan 2019 (“POSP”), collectively referred to as “the Plans”. The conditional shares that were granted under the Plans on October 13, 202 have now vested.

data.title: Anthony Scaramucci stood by Bitcoin despite business risks amid 2022 Crypto Winter, when BTC dropped from $69,000 to $16,000.
data.description: During a recent podcast, Anthony Scaramucci, CEO of SkyBridge Capital, highlighted his strong conviction in Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) despite facing challenges in his business. Scaramucci discussed the 2022 cryptocurrency winter when Bitcoin's value dropped significantly from $69,000 to $16,000, impacting his New York-based firm. He mentioned losing numerous clients and being depicted negatively in the New York Post. Despite the setbacks, Scaramucci remained optimistic about Bitcoin's potential. To read more about his reflections, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Weekly recap of 11 finance press releases, covering tariffs impact on holiday spending, PayPal's cybersecurity tips, and sports fan spending habits. NEW YORK, Oct. 17, 2025.
data.description: This week's top financial updates from PR Newswire in New York highlight the impact of tariffs on holiday shopping, cybersecurity advice from PayPal, and a study on the spending behaviors of sports enthusiasts. Released on October 17, 2025, these reports delve into pressing issues in the financial world. Notably, the discussion around tariffs is influencing consumer sentiment and could shape holiday spending patterns. Additionally, PayPal shared crucial cybersecurity tips to safeguard users against online threats. Moreover, an analysis of sports fans' spending habits sheds light on their purchasing preferences and trends. These releases offer valuable insights into current economic trends and consumer behaviors.

data.title: France intensifies scrutiny on Binance and others amid EU power struggle as ACPR audits dozens of exchanges to enforce MiCA regulations.
data.description: The French regulatory authority ACPR is conducting audits on Binance and several other exchanges as part of Paris' efforts to play a more significant role in enforcing MiCA regulations throughout Europe.
